dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9549 IH: To amend title 10, United States Code, the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022, and the Military Construction Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2018 to repeal certain reporting requirements related to unfunded priorities, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Jayapal (for herself, Mr. Davidson, Mr. Garamendi, and Mr. Moulton) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To amend title 10, United States Code, the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022, and the Military Construction Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2018 to repeal certain reporting requirements related to unfunded priorities, and for other purposes.

section.header: Repealing reporting requirements related to unfunded priorities

subsection.text: Title 10, United States Code, is amended by repealing each of the following sections:

subsection.header: Small business innovation research and technology transfer program
subsection.text: Section 865 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022 (Public law 117–81; 10 U.S.C. 4901 note) is repealed.
Section 2806 of the Military Construction Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2018 (division B of Public Law 115–91; 10 U.S.C. 222a note) is repealed.
